title: RapidMeta Infectious Disease | Malaria Vaccines NMA (RTS,S + R21 / Matrix-M) v1.0
hero_h2: Malaria Vaccines for African Children: RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M

pico.pop: African children residing in P. falciparum malaria-endemic areas; primary cohorts age 5-17 months at first vaccination (RTS,S Phase 3, R21 Phase 3) or 5-36 months (R21 Phase 2b)
pico.int: Pre-erythrocytic CSP-based malaria vaccine: RTS,S/AS01 (Mosquirix; AS01 adjuvant) or R21/Matrix-M (Matrix-M adjuvant), 3-dose primary series followed by booster
pico.comp: Comparator vaccine (rabies in RTS,S P3; rabies / control in R21 trials) with same schedule
pico.out: Clinical falciparum malaria episode (case definition: parasitaemia + symptoms; first or any episode within 12-month follow-up). Reported here as per-protocol incidence-rate / hazard ratio.
pico.subgroup: Age cohort (5-17 mo vs older), country / transmission intensity, seasonal vs perennial transmission, vaccine schedule (3-dose vs 3+booster), HIV / sickle cell status

real_data_entries.name: RTS,S Phase 3
real_data_entries.group: RTS,S/AS01 4-dose schedule (3-dose primary + 18-month booster) vs rabies-vaccine control in children 5-17 months at first dose; multi-country (Burkina Faso, Gabon, Ghana, Kenya, Malawi, Mozambique, Tanzania); cumulative incidence to month 48

allOutcomes.title: Clinical malaria episode by month 48 (per-protocol primary, IRR)

real_data_entries.snippet: Source: RTS,S Clinical Trials Partnership. Lancet 2015;386:31-45 (RTS,S/AS01 phase 3 final report).

real_data_entries.name: R21 Phase 2b
real_data_entries.group: R21/Matrix-M (Matrix-M 50 ug high-dose adjuvant) 3-dose primary series vs rabies-vaccine control in children 5-17 months in Burkina Faso (Nanoro); 12-month follow-up

allOutcomes.title: Clinical malaria episode at 12 months (vaccine efficacy 77%, high-dose adjuvant arm)

real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Datoo MS et al. Lancet 2021;397:1809-1818 (R21/Matrix-M phase 2b Burkina Faso).

real_data_entries.name: R21 Phase 3
real_data_entries.group: R21/Matrix-M 3-dose primary + booster vs rabies-vaccine control; multi-country (Burkina Faso, Kenya, Mali, Tanzania); 12-month efficacy in children 5-36 months

allOutcomes.title: Clinical malaria episode at 12 months (vaccine efficacy 75% all sites)

real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Datoo MS et al. Lancet 2024;403:533-544 (R21/Matrix-M phase 3 multi-country).
